title: PubChem Vocabulary
description: Key terms, identifiers, and concepts used in the PubChem API.

terms.label: Compound ID
terms.definition: PubChem Compound Identifier — a unique integer assigned to each distinct chemical structure in PubChem. CIDs are canonical and stable.

terms.label: Substance ID
terms.definition: PubChem Substance Identifier — a unique integer assigned to each substance record submitted to PubChem by a depositor. Multiple SIDs may map to a single CID.

terms.label: Assay ID
terms.definition: PubChem BioAssay Identifier — a unique integer identifying a bioassay experiment record in PubChem BioAssay.

terms.label: Simplified Molecular-Input Line-Entry System
terms.definition: A notation that allows a user to represent a chemical structure in a way that can be used by the computer. PubChem supports both canonical and isomeric SMILES.

terms.label: International Chemical Identifier
terms.definition: A textual identifier for chemical substances, designed to provide a standard way to encode molecular information. Developed by IUPAC.

terms.label: InChI Key
terms.definition: A fixed-length (27-character) condensed digital representation of the InChI. Designed for web searching and indexing.

terms.label: Molecular Formula
terms.definition: Chemical formula expressing the types and numbers of atoms in a molecule using element symbols and counts.

terms.label: Molecular Weight
terms.definition: The average mass of a molecule calculated using the atomic weights of each constituent element, expressed in g/mol.

terms.label: IUPAC Name
terms.definition: Systematic chemical name following the nomenclature recommendations of the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry.

terms.label: XLogP3
terms.definition: Computed partition coefficient (log P) between octanol and water, calculated using the XLogP3 method. Indicates lipophilicity.

terms.label: Topological Polar Surface Area
terms.definition: The surface sum over all polar atoms (oxygen, nitrogen, and their attached hydrogens) in a molecule, expressed in square angstroms. Related to oral bioavailability.

terms.label: Hydrogen Bond Donor Count
terms.definition: Number of nitrogen-hydrogen (NH) and oxygen-hydrogen (OH) bonds in a compound. Relevant to drug-likeness (Lipinski Rule of Five).

terms.label: Hydrogen Bond Acceptor Count
terms.definition: Number of nitrogen (N) and oxygen (O) atoms in a compound. Relevant to drug-likeness (Lipinski Rule of Five).

terms.label: List Key
terms.definition: A temporary server-side key returned by asynchronous search operations (substructure, superstructure, similarity). Used to poll for and retrieve results.

terms.label: Power User Gateway
terms.definition: PubChem's primary programmatic interface supporting batch downloads, structure searches, and data retrieval. Available as both REST (PUG REST) and SOAP (PUG SOAP) interfaces.

terms.label: Structure Data File
terms.definition: A chemical file format that allows multiple chemical structures and associated data to be archived in a single file. One of the output formats supported by PubChem APIs.

terms.label: Tanimoto Coefficient
terms.definition: A similarity metric used in chemical structure comparison based on the ratio of shared features to total features. Used in the PubChem 2D similarity search, with scores from 0 (no similarity) to 100 (identical).
